description: E2E test: add 'curate count' subcommand that prints learning statistics (total, active, retired, embedded).

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
principles: Keep it simple — this is a small feature
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

acceptanceCriteria: CurateAction::Count variant added to CLI enum
Handler queries: total learnings, active (retired_at IS NULL), retired (retired_at IS NOT NULL), embedded (JOIN learning_embeddings)
Output formatted as a table: Total: N, Active: N, Retired: N, Embedded: N
Unit test: count_learnings_stats returns correct counts with mixed active/retired/embedded learnings
Integration: `task-mgr curate count` runs without error on an empty DB
userStories.description: Add a 'curate count' subcommand that queries the database and prints learning statistics: total, active, retired, and embedded counts. Follow the existing CurateAction pattern.
userStories.notes: Follow the Retire/Unretire/Dedup pattern. The count queries are simple SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM learnings, SELECT COUNT(*) FROM learnings WHERE retired_at IS NULL, SELECT COUNT(*) FROM learnings WHERE retired_at IS NOT NULL, SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT le.learning_id) FROM learning_embeddings le JOIN learnings l ON l.id = le.learning_id WHERE l.retired_at IS NULL.

correctness: Counts must be accurate for mixed active/retired/embedded learnings
